solution:
```cpp
#include <string>
#include <unordered_map>

using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    int nextBeautifulNumber(int n) {
        for (int x = n + 1; ; ++x) {
            string s = to_string(x);
            unordered_map<char, int> freq;
            for (char c : s) {
                if (c != '0') {
                    freq[c]++;
                }
            }
            bool valid = true;
            for (char c : s) {
                if (c == '0') {
                    valid = false;
                    break;
                }
                int d = c - '0';
                if (freq[c] != d) {
                    valid = false;
                    break;
                }
            }
            if (valid) {
                return x;
            }
        }
    }
};
```

efficiency: MEDIUM

time complexity: O(k), where k is the number of numbers checked until the next balanced number is found.

space complexity: O(1), as the frequency map size is bounded by the number of digits (at most 10).

energy implications: MEDIUM

explanation: The solution iterates through each number greater than n, checking if it meets the balanced condition by ensuring each digit d appears exactly d times and no zeros are present. This approach is straightforward but may require checking many numbers in the worst case, though it's efficient enough for the given constraints.
